---
title: "R Notebook"
output: html_notebook
---

This is an [R Markdown](http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com) Notebook. When you execute code within the notebook, the results appear beneath the code. 

Try executing this chunk by clicking the *Run* button within the chunk or by placing your cursor inside it and pressing *Cmd+Shift+Enter*. 

### Install and Load packages 
```{r}
source("microarray_packages.R")
```

### Importing Normalised Data 
```{r}
gse <- getGEO("GSE25504", GSEMatrix = TRUE)
```
```{r}
names(gse)
sapply(gse, function(x) ncol(exprs(x)))
```
Total 170 samples analysed in 4 different platforms, for training the model we are using -"GPL6947	Illumina HumanHT-12 V3.0 expression beadchip" as mentioned in the original papers - PMID: 25120092 and PMID: 26484146

```{r}
sampleinfo <- pData(gse[[4]])
feature_data <- fData(gse[[4]])
expr_matrix <- exprs(gse[[4]])
dim(expr_matrix)
```

```{r}
summary(as.vector(expr_matrix))
```
The expression matrix checked with QC -> RSN normalisation and quality control steps applied  
```{r}
#Check for any duplicated data 
list(
  dim = dim(expr_matrix),
  duplicated_rows = sum(duplicated(expr_matrix)),
  duplicated_cols = sum(duplicated(t(expr_matrix))),
  duplicated_rownames = sum(duplicated(rownames(expr_matrix))),
  duplicated_colnames = sum(duplicated(colnames(expr_matrix)))
)
```

### Exploratory Data Analysis 
```{r}
#To visualise IQR - middle 50% of data spread, removing extreme outlier ends of data - QC
boxplot(expr_matrix, outline = FALSE, las = 2)
```
```{r}
# Convert expression matrix to long format
expr_viol <- expr_matrix %>%
  as_tibble(rownames = "Gene") %>%
  pivot_longer(-Gene, names_to = "Sample", values_to = "Value")

# Create sample chunks (3–4 panels)
n_samples <- length(unique(expr_viol$Sample))
n_chunks <- 4 #no of panels
chunk_size <- ceiling(n_samples / n_chunks)

expr_viol <- expr_viol %>%
  mutate(chunk = rep(1:n_chunks, each = chunk_size, length.out = n_samples)[match(Sample, unique(Sample))])

expr_viol$chunk <- factor(expr_viol$chunk)

# Violin plots, one panel per chunk
ggplot(expr_viol, aes(x = Sample, y = Value)) +
  geom_violin(alpha = 0.5, fill = "skyblue") +
  geom_point(data = expr_viol %>% sample_frac(0.1), 
             alpha = 0.05, 
             position = position_jitter(seed = 1, width = 0.2)) +
  facet_wrap(~chunk, scales = "free_x") +  # separate plots for each chunk
  theme(legend.position = "none") +
  xlab("") +
  ylab("log2(mRNA fluorescence intensity)") +
  ggtitle("mRNA probe intensity distribution of all the samples") +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5),
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1, vjust = 0.5))
```
### Post-QC data preprocessing
```{r}
# --- Filtering pipeline -----------

# 1) Remove low-variance probes (bottom 20%)
gene_var <- apply(expr_matrix, 1, var, na.rm = TRUE)
var_threshold <- quantile(gene_var, 0.2, na.rm = TRUE)
keep_var <- gene_var > var_threshold
expr_var_filtered <- expr_matrix[keep_var, ]

cat("After variance filtering:", dim(expr_var_filtered), "\n")
# --- Build probe annotation table ---
# FROM FEATURE DATA
# probe_annotation <- data.frame(
#   ProbeID = rownames(feature_data),
#   GeneSymbol = feature_data[["Symbol"]])

# Suppose your probe IDs are rownames of your expression matrix
probe_ids <- rownames(expr_var_filtered)  

# Map probe IDs to gene symbols using org.Hs.eg.db
# Adjust 'keytype' to your platform, e.g., "PROBEID" for Affymetrix, "ENSEMBL" for Ensembl IDs
# Map to gene symbols
gene_symbols <- mapIds(illuminaHumanv4.db,
                       keys = probe_ids,
                       column = "SYMBOL",   # gene symbol
                       keytype = "PROBEID",
                       multiVals = "first")  # take first if multiple mapping

# Create probe_annotation data frame
probe_annotation <- data.frame(
  ProbeID = probe_ids,
  GeneSymbol = gene_symbols,
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)
# 2) Remove unannotated probes: 16k
keep_annot <- !is.na(probe_annotation$GeneSymbol[match(rownames(expr_var_filtered), probe_annotation$ProbeID)]) #Filter out unmatched probes and matched probes with no gene symbol

expr_anno <- expr_var_filtered[keep_annot, ]
cat("After removing unannotated probes:", dim(expr_anno), "\n")

# 3) Collapse duplicates by max expression: 11k
expr_collapsed <- expr_var_filtered %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  mutate(
    ProbeID = rownames(.),
    GeneSymbol = probe_annotation$GeneSymbol[match(ProbeID, probe_annotation$ProbeID)],
    # Use GeneSymbol if available, otherwise fallback to ProbeID
    GeneSymbol_final = ifelse(is.na(GeneSymbol) | GeneSymbol == "",
                              ProbeID,
                              GeneSymbol)
  ) %>%
  group_by(GeneSymbol_final) %>%
  summarise(across(where(is.numeric), max, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  column_to_rownames("GeneSymbol_final")

cat("After adjusting for multiple probes to gene matching:", dim(expr_collapsed), "\n")
# 4) CV filtering 
# Linearize
expr_linear <- 2^expr_collapsed

# Compute CV per gene
cv <- apply(expr_linear, 1, sd) / apply(expr_linear, 1, mean)

# Filter genes with CV > 0.1
filtered_genes <- expr_collapsed[cv > 0.1, ]  # Keep log2 values for downstream

cat("After CV filtering:", dim(filtered_genes), "\n")
```
```{r}
filtered_genes <- filtered_genes[!grepl("^ILMN_", rownames(filtered_genes)), ]
```

### Creating new sample subset with interested factors  
```{r}
sampleinfo_new <- sampleinfo %>% 
  select(title, characteristics_ch1, characteristics_ch1.1, characteristics_ch1.3, characteristics_ch1.6, characteristics_ch1.7,characteristics_ch1.4, `other positive results:ch1`) %>%
  extract(
    col = characteristics_ch1.6, 
    into = c("Gestation_weeks", "Neonate_days"),
    regex = ".*: (\\d+)\\+(\\d+)$",
    convert = TRUE, 
    remove = TRUE
  ) %>% rename(other_indications = `other positive results:ch1`) %>% 
  rename(blood_test_results = characteristics_ch1.4) %>% 
  rename(disease_status = characteristics_ch1) %>%
  mutate(disease_status = sub("^neonate: ", "", disease_status)) %>%
  rename(gender = characteristics_ch1.1) %>%
  mutate(gender = sub("^gender: ", "", gender)) %>%
  rename(Antibiotics = characteristics_ch1.3) %>%
  mutate(Antibiotics = sub("^on antibiotics at time of sample: ", "", Antibiotics)) %>%
  rename(Birthweight = characteristics_ch1.7) %>%
  mutate(Birthweight = sub("^birthweight \\(g\\):\\s*", "", Birthweight)) 
str(sampleinfo_new)  
```

```{r}
# Define the GSM IDs you want to keep as "infected"
specific_infected <- c("Inf009", "Inf047", "Inf075")

sampleinfo_new <- sampleinfo_new %>%
  mutate(disease_status = case_when(
    sampleinfo_new$title %in% specific_infected ~ "other_infected",  # specific GSMs
    TRUE ~ disease_status                                     # keep everything else as is
  ))
```

```{r}
sampleinfo_new <- sampleinfo_new %>% 
  select(title, disease_status, Gestation_weeks, Neonate_days, Birthweight, gender, Antibiotics, blood_test_results, other_indications) %>%
  mutate(
    disease_status = factor(disease_status),
    Antibiotics = factor(Antibiotics),
    gender = factor(gender),
    Birthweight = as.numeric(Birthweight),
    Gestation_weeks = as.numeric(Gestation_weeks),
    Neonate_days = as.numeric(Neonate_days),
    # Create binary columns
    Term = factor(ifelse(Gestation_weeks >= 37, "term", "preterm")),
    Weight = factor(ifelse(Birthweight > 2500, "NBW", "LBW")),
    Corrected_GA = Gestation_weeks + (Neonate_days/7), 
  )

str(sampleinfo_new) 
```
### sample data visualisation

```{r}
# Create a matrix of counts
df_matrix <- sampleinfo_new %>%
  group_by(disease_status, Term, Weight) %>%
  summarise(count = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = Weight, values_from = count, values_fill = 0)  # columns = Weights

# Convert to matrix for clustering
mat <- as.matrix(df_matrix[,-c(1:2)])  # remove disease_status & Term columns
rownames(mat) <- paste(df_matrix$disease_status, df_matrix$Term, sep = "_")

mat

pheatmap(mat,
         cluster_rows = FALSE,
         cluster_cols = FALSE,
         display_numbers = TRUE,     # show counts in cells
         main = "Distribution of Samples with respect to GA, BW, disease status",
         color = colorRampPalette(c("white", "skyblue", "steelblue"))(50))
```

### Unsupervised PCA Analysis

```{r}
# PCA
pca <- prcomp(t(filtered_genes), scale. = TRUE)

# Compute variance explained
var_explained <- pca$sdev^2 / sum(pca$sdev^2) * 100

sum(var_explained[1:3])
# Disease status - Plot PC1 vs PC2 with % variance
plot(pca$x[,1], pca$x[,2],
     col = sampleinfo_new$disease_status,
     pch = 16,
     xlab = paste0("PC1 (", round(var_explained[1], 1), "%)"),
     ylab = paste0("PC2 (", round(var_explained[2], 1), "%)"),
     main = "PCA of samples with genes CV > 0.1")
legend("topright", legend = levels(sampleinfo_new$disease_status), col = 1:length(levels(sampleinfo_new$disease_status)), pch=16)
```
```{r}
# --- Scree plot ---
# Base R version
plot(var_explained, type = "b", pch = 19,
     xlab = "Principal Component",
     ylab = "Variance Explained (%)",
     main = "Scree Plot")
abline(h=5, col="gray", lty=2)

```
### Differentially Expression analysis

```{r}
design <- model.matrix(~ 0 + disease_status, data = sampleinfo_new)
colnames(design) <- levels(sampleinfo_new$disease_status)
# design
colnames(design)
```

```{r}
fit <- lmFit(filtered_genes, design)
contrast.matrix <- makeContrasts(infected - control, levels = design)
fit2 <- contrasts.fit(fit, contrast.matrix)
fit2 <- eBayes(fit2)
```

```{r}
top_genes <- topTable(fit2, number = Inf, adjust.method = "BH")
head(top_genes)
```

```{r}
sum(top_genes$adj.P.Val <= 0.01)
sum(top_genes$adj.P.Val <= 0.01 & abs(top_genes$logFC) >= 1)
sig_genes <- top_genes %>% filter(adj.P.Val <= 0.01 & abs(logFC) >= 1) 
```


### PCA Analysis after Statistical testing

```{r}
# PCA
pca <- prcomp(t(filtered_genes[rownames(sig_genes), ]), scale. = TRUE)

# Compute variance explained
var_explained <- pca$sdev^2 / sum(pca$sdev^2) * 100

sum(var_explained[1:3])

# Disease status - Plot PC1 vs PC2 with % variance
plot(pca$x[,1], pca$x[,2],
     col = sampleinfo_new$disease_status,
     pch = 16,
     xlab = paste0("PC1 (", round(var_explained[1], 1), "%)"),
     ylab = paste0("PC2 (", round(var_explained[2], 1), "%)"),
     main = "Supervised PCA after P-value cutoff")
legend("bottomleft", legend = levels(sampleinfo_new$disease_status), col = 1:length(levels(sampleinfo_new$disease_status)), pch=16)
```
```{r}
# Removing viral infection sample - GSM627045
filtered_genes <- filtered_genes[, colnames(filtered_genes) != "GSM627045"]
sampleinfo_new <- sampleinfo_new[rownames(sampleinfo_new) != "GSM627045", ]
```

```{r}
# PCA
pca <- prcomp(t(filtered_genes[rownames(sig_genes), ]), scale. = TRUE)

# Compute variance explained
var_explained <- pca$sdev^2 / sum(pca$sdev^2) * 100

sum(var_explained[1:3])
# Disease status - Plot PC1 vs PC2 with % variance
plot(pca$x[,1], pca$x[,2],
     col = sampleinfo_new$disease_status,
     pch = 16,
     xlab = paste0("PC1 (", round(var_explained[1], 1), "%)"),
     ylab = paste0("PC2 (", round(var_explained[2], 1), "%)"),
     main = "Supervised PCA after P-value cutoff")
legend("topright", legend = levels(sampleinfo_new$disease_status), col = 1:length(levels(sampleinfo_new$disease_status)), pch=16)
```
### Differentially Expression analysis, adjusting for Birthweight 

```{r}
sampleinfo_new$Birthweight_kg <- sampleinfo_new$Birthweight / 1000
design_1 <- model.matrix(~ 0 + Birthweight_kg, data = sampleinfo_new)

# design
colnames(design_1)
```

```{r}
fit3 <- lmFit(filtered_genes, design_1)
# contrast.matrix <- makeContrasts(disease_statusinfected - disease_statuscontrol, levels = design_1)
# fit4 <- contrasts.fit(fit3, contrast.matrix)
fit4 <- eBayes(fit3)
```

```{r}
top_genes_BW <- topTable(fit4, coef = 1, number = Inf, adjust.method = "BH")
head(top_genes_BW)
```
```{r}
sum(top_genes_BW$adj.P.Val <= 0.00001)
sum(top_genes_BW$adj.P.Val <= 0.00001 & abs(top_genes_BW$logFC) >= 2)
sig_genes_BW <- top_genes_BW %>% filter(adj.P.Val <= 0.01 & abs(logFC) >= log2(1.25))
```
```{r}
# PCA
pca <- prcomp(t(filtered_genes[rownames(sig_genes_BW), ]), scale. = TRUE)

# Compute variance explained
var_explained <- pca$sdev^2 / sum(pca$sdev^2) * 100

sum(var_explained[1:3])
# Disease status - Plot PC1 vs PC2 with % variance
plot(pca$x[,1], pca$x[,2],
     col = sampleinfo_new$disease_status,
     pch = 16,
     xlab = paste0("PC1 (", round(var_explained[1], 1), "%)"),
     ylab = paste0("PC2 (", round(var_explained[2], 1), "%)"),
     main = "Supervised PCA after P-value cutoff")
legend("topright", legend = levels(sampleinfo_new$disease_status), col = 1:length(levels(sampleinfo_new$disease_status)), pch=16)
```

### Volcano plot with DE genes correlated to Birthweight 

```{r}
# Add a column to flag significant genes
top_genes_BW$Significant <- with(top_genes_BW, adj.P.Val <= 0.01 & abs(logFC) >= log2(1.25))

# Basic volcano plot
ggplot(top_genes_BW, aes(x = logFC, y = -log10(adj.P.Val))) +
  geom_point(aes(color = Significant), alpha = 0.6) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("black", "red")) +
  theme_minimal() +
  xlab("Log2 Fold Change") +
  ylab("-Log10 Adjusted P-Value") +
  ggtitle("Volcano Plot of Differentially Expressed Genes correlated to Birthweight") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = c(-log2(1.25), log2(1.25)), linetype = "dashed", color = "blue") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = -log10(0.01), linetype = "dashed", color = "blue")
```
### Over-Representation Analysis - BW linearly correlated to total population  

```{r}
sig_genes_ORA <- sig_genes_BW
sig_genes_ORA <- rownames(sig_genes_ORA)
valid_genes <- sig_genes_ORA[!grepl("^ILMN_", sig_genes_ORA)]
gene_entrez <- bitr(valid_genes,
                    fromType = "SYMBOL",
                    toType = "ENTREZID",
                    OrgDb = org.Hs.eg.db)

gene_entrez <- gene_entrez$ENTREZID
```

```{r}

# KEGG pathways
kegg_res <- enrichKEGG(gene = gene_entrez,
                       pAdjustMethod="BH",
                       organism = 'hsa',     # human
                       pvalueCutoff = 0.05)
# Reactome pathways
reactome_res <- enrichPathway(gene = gene_entrez,
                              organism = "human",
                              pAdjustMethod="BH",
                              pvalueCutoff = 0.05,
                              readable = TRUE)
go_res <- enrichGO(gene=gene_entrez,
                   OrgDb=org.Hs.eg.db,
                   ont="BP",
                   pAdjustMethod="BH",
                   pvalueCutoff=0.05,
                   readable=TRUE)
```


```{r}
barplot(reactome_res, showCategory=10, title="Top Reactome Pathways in Sepsis population linearly correlated to BW")
barplot(go_res, showCategory=10, title="GO Biological Process Enrichment in Sepsis population linearly correlated to BW")
barplot(kegg_res, showCategory=10, title="Top KEGG Pathways in Sepsis population linearly correlated to BW")
```

```{r}
# ===============================
# Script: Extract Human Innate Immune System Pathways & Genes
# ===============================
# ---- 1. Convert enrichResult to data frame ----
reactome_df <- as.data.frame(reactome_res)

# ---- 2. Convert Reactome pathways to proper data frame ----
all_pathways_list <- as.list(reactomePATHID2NAME)

all_pathways_df <- data.frame(
  PATHID = names(all_pathways_list),         # pathway IDs
  PATHNAME = unlist(all_pathways_list),      # pathway names
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)
# ---- 3. Define keywords for innate immune system ----
keywords <- c(
  "innate immune system",
  "toll-like receptor",
  "cytokine",
  "pattern recognition receptor",
  "interferon",
  "neutrophil",
  "dendritic",
  "NF-kappaB", 
  "inflammasome", 
  "NOD",
  "chemokines"
)

# ---- 4. Identify all relevant pathways ----
# Only keep pathways whose names match the keywords
innate_hierarchy <- all_pathways_df %>%
  filter(grepl(paste(keywords, collapse = "|"), PATHNAME, ignore.case = TRUE))

# Only consider pathways that are in human enrichment results
innate_pathway_ids <- intersect(innate_hierarchy$PATHID, reactome_df$ID)

# ---- 5. Filter enrichment results for innate immune system pathways ----
innate_df <- reactome_df %>%
  filter(ID %in% innate_pathway_ids)
innate_df <- innate_df %>% filter(innate_df$Description != "Mitotic Telophase/Cytokinesis")
# ---- 6. Extract genes ----
# Each pathway has genes separated by "/"
innate_genes <- unique(unlist(strsplit(innate_df$geneID, "/")))

# ---- 7. Optional: Create pathway → gene mapping ----
innate_pathway_genes <- innate_df %>%
  select(Description, geneID) %>%
  mutate(Genes = strsplit(geneID, "/")) %>%
  select(-geneID)

# ---- 8. Output summary ----
cat("Number of enriched human innate immune system pathways:", nrow(innate_df), "\n")
cat("Number of unique genes in these pathways:", length(innate_genes), "\n")

# ---- 9. Results ----
# Pathway enrichment table
innate_df

# Unique genes
innate_genes

# Pathway-to-gene mapping
innate_pathway_genes
```
```{r}
library(dplyr)

# Filter the pathway in your innate_df
neutrophil_pathway <- innate_df %>%
  filter(grepl("neutrophil degranulation", Description, ignore.case = TRUE))

# Extract gene symbols
neutrophil_genes <- unique(unlist(strsplit(neutrophil_pathway$geneID, "/")))

head(neutrophil_genes)
```
```{r}
# Assuming expr_mat has gene symbols as rownames
expr_neusubset <- filtered_genes[rownames(filtered_genes) %in% neutrophil_genes, ]

# Match the order of columns in expr_neusubset
colnames(expr_neusubset) <- sampleinfo_new[colnames(expr_neusubset), "title"]
# Check
head(expr_neusubset)
```

```{r}
# Pick top 100 most sig genes
sig_genes_BW <- sig_genes_BW[!grepl("^ILMN_", rownames(sig_genes_BW)), ]
# Convert rownames to a column
sig_genes_clean <- sig_genes_BW %>%
  rownames_to_column(var = "Gene")

# Keep only genes present in your neutrophil list
neutro_sig <- sig_genes_clean %>%
  filter(Gene %in% neutrophil_genes)

#Top 20 neutrophil related genes
top_neutro_genes <- neutro_sig %>%
  mutate(absLogFC = abs(logFC)) %>%
  arrange(desc(absLogFC)) %>%
  slice(1:10) %>%
  pull(Gene)

expr_subset_sig <- expr_neusubset[
  rownames(expr_neusubset) %in% top_neutro_genes, 
]

expr_long <- as.data.frame(expr_subset_sig) %>%
  rownames_to_column("Gene") %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = -Gene,
    names_to = "Sample",
    values_to = "Expression"
  )

ggplot(expr_long, aes(x = Sample, y = Expression, color = Gene, group = Gene)) +
  geom_line(size = 1) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Top 20 Neutrophil DE Genes (by |logFC|)",
    x = "Sample",
    y = "Normalized Expression"
  ) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1))
```
